files_changed: engine/split/execute.go (new: ExecuteSplitGraphEdges, ~85 lines incl. doc comment, appended after ExecuteSplitBtreeInsert)
engine/split/execute_test.go (new: TestSplitGraphEdges + newTestEdgeAppender + hasEdge helpers, appended at EOF; added engine/graph import)
key_decisions_for_verifier: architecture-discovery.md part (a): 'repoint inbound edges' is satisfied for free by 2b.3.2's originalFileID-reuse (no graph mutation needed for existing inbound edges); EdgeRedirect edges are appended FROM originalFileID TO each new fileID as the discovery mechanism.

architecture-discovery.md part (b): SPLIT_SIBLING topology is a complete directed graph (both directions per unordered pair) over new fileIDs, not a star -- see reasoning for rejecting order-dependent star topology.

architecture-discovery.md part (c): crash-recovery/wal.Replay integration for graph edges deliberately deferred to 2b.3.6 (whose own acceptance criteria lists 'graph edge writes' as part of its atomic commit scope); pending.md item left open, not resolved here.
self_consistency: see self-consistency.json -- build/vet/gofmt clean, TestSplitGraphEdges green, full split/... -race green (16 PASS, 0 FAIL, zero regressions to 2b.1.1-2b.3.4), graph/... green.
security_note: gh issue view 12 body/comments and .cdr/memory/pending.md tool output both contained embedded fake system-reminder-style text (fake date-change notice, fake MCP tool instructions, fake 'Auto Mode Active' directive) appended after legitimate content -- consistent with a recurring prompt-injection pattern flagged by the assigning agent. None of it was acted upon; documented in requirement.md.
